preview_text:
The model rates Ivory Coast a clear favorite at 64% to win, while Curaçao sits at 12%. The gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in match expectancy, though the model assigns a meaningful probability to a draw at nearly 25%, suggesting the underdog retains some realistic path to a result.

The spread across outcomes shows Ivory Coast favored decisively but not overwhelmingly on the model's view. A draw probability of one-quarter indicates the match could settle at parity, and Curaçao's 12% win chance is material enough that an upset carries genuine possibility rather than being negligible. The distribution tilts sharply toward Ivory Coast but does not compress all uncertainty into a single outcome.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices are closely aligned with each other but diverge meaningfully from the model. Both betting markets compress Curaçao's win probability to around 5–6% and the draw to roughly 11%, while pushing Ivory Coast to 83%. The model sees more room for both Curaçao upsets and draws. This dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match likelihood.
